settings.title: Settings
settings.sub: Requirements, your memory repo, and how it syncs.
settings.requirementsSub: Claude Total Recall syncs your memory through a private GitHub repo, using git and the GitHub CLI (gh). Both need to be installed, and gh signed in to your GitHub account.
settings.repoSub: The private GitHub repo that stores your synced memory — one repo, shared by all your machines.
settings.autoSyncSub: Pushes instantly when files change and pulls from the repo periodically. Runs while the app is open.
settings.aboutText: Claude Total Recall v{{version}} — syncs Claude Code memory across your machines via a private GitHub repo.

home.title: Your memory, synced on its own
home.sub: Claude Total Recall keeps Claude Code's memory up to date across your machines: it pushes the moment you edit and pulls from the repo every few minutes. Nothing to press.
home.conflictCard_one: Sync is paused by {{count}} conflict. Resolve it so it keeps going on its own.
home.conflictCard_other: Sync is paused by {{count}} conflicts. Resolve them so it keeps going on its own.
home.firstProject: Add your first project to sync specific folders?

about.title: About Claude Total Recall
about.body: Syncs Claude Code memory across your machines via a private GitHub repo.

sync.resolveBeforeIncoming: Resolve the conflicts before the incoming sync: {{conflicts}}
sync.integrateConflicts: Conflicts while integrating: {{conflicts}}. Resolve them below.

planReview.title: Review changes
planReview.nothing: Nothing to do: everything is in sync.

preview.sourceUnavailable: This file has no source on this machine to open.

machine.notRegisteredAddProject: Machine not registered; register before adding projects.
machine.notRegisteredLocal: Machine not registered (local.json missing). Run `claude-total-recall register`.
machine.notRegisteredPrefs: Machine not registered; nowhere to save preferences.

push.retriesExhaustedRegister: Could not register after several retries (push rejected).
push.retriesExhausted: Could not save after several retries.

project.invalidName: Invalid project name: "{{value}}". Use only letters, numbers, dot, hyphen and underscore.
project.folderNested: This folder overlaps "{{conflict}}" ({{where}}), already synced on this machine. Nesting would sync the same files twice.
project.existsCaseInsensitive: A project named "{{existing}}" already exists (names are case-insensitive).

pin.invalidName: Invalid pinned-file name: "{{value}}". Use only letters, numbers, dot, hyphen and underscore.
pin.folderNested: This file overlaps "{{conflict}}" ({{where}}), already synced on this machine.

slot.invalidName: Invalid slot name: "{{value}}". Use only letters, numbers, dot, hyphen and underscore.

platform.unsupported: Unsupported platform: {{platform}}. Claude Total Recall supports macOS and Linux (Windows later).

plan.expired: Plan expired; rebuild it (the preview is no longer valid).

notebook.exists: A Notebook entry already exists at "{{value}}".
notebook.invalidMove: Cannot move an entry into itself.
notebook.repoConflict: Resolve the pending sync conflicts before editing the Notebook.

sync.pendingConflict: Resolve the pending sync conflicts before continuing.

ghAuth.fix: Run: gh auth login && gh auth setup-git
ghAuth.noGh: gh is not installed, cannot verify auth

constellation.overview: Constellation of machines around the repo.
constellation.upToDate: Machine {{machineId}}: up to date with the repo.

planDrift.title: The disk changed
planDrift.body_one: Since you built the preview, {{count}} file changed on disk. The plan you confirmed no longer matches what's there now.
planDrift.body_other: Since you built the preview, {{count}} files changed on disk. The plan you confirmed no longer matches what's there now.
planDrift.help: Rebuild builds a fresh preview (recommended). Force applies the old plan anyway.

projects.title: Projects
projects.sub: A project groups memory folders; each folder's path is stored literally per machine.
projects.registerMachineHint: You need to register this computer before assigning folders.
projects.noProjectsHint: Create one to sync specific memory folders.
projects.unassociatedTitle_one: {{count}} project needs configuring on this machine
projects.unassociatedTitle_other: {{count}} projects need configuring on this machine
projects.unassociatedHint: Open one and use “Configure on this machine” to link its local folder — instead of creating a new one.

deleteProject.title: Delete project
deleteProject.body: Delete "{{name}}" for all machines? It doesn't remove your local folders, only the sync configuration.

projects.noSourcesHint: Add a folder or file so this project syncs on this machine.

removeFolder.title: Remove folder
removeFolder.body: Remove the slot "{{slot}}" from this machine? It doesn't delete the folder, it just stops syncing it here.

projects.alreadyExists: A project with that name already exists. Assign it a path on its card.
projects.projectNameHint: Links the project across your machines (letters, numbers, . _ -).

folderRedirect.adjusted: Adjusted to the {{leaf}}/ subfolder — this looks like a project root that contains it.

project.nameEmpty: The project name can't be empty.
project.nameInvalid: Invalid project name. Use only letters, numbers, dot, hyphen and underscore.

slot.nameEmpty: The slot name can't be empty.
slot.nameInvalid: Invalid slot name. Use only letters, numbers, dot, hyphen and underscore.

discover.title: Add a project from a folder
discover.nothingFound: No Claude memory found in this folder.
discover.maybeExisting: This looks like the existing project “{{name}}”. Associate it here instead of creating a new one?

adopt.title: Configure "{{name}}" on this machine
adopt.manual: Couldn't map automatically — pick a path
adopt.pickLocal: Claude names this dir differently here — pick the local one
adopt.candidates: Local Claude dirs that might match:
adopt.noReference: No other machine has this source
adopt.selectOne: Select at least one source with a path.
adopt.empty: Nothing to configure — every source already has a path here.

new.title: Add a project
new.unassociatedHint_one: {{count}} project from another machine isn’t associated here yet — if this folder is it, you’ll be offered to associate instead of creating a duplicate.
new.unassociatedHint_other: {{count}} projects from other machines aren’t associated here yet — if this folder is one of them, you’ll be offered to associate instead of creating a duplicate.
new.scanHint: Find projects under ~/.claude/projects and create them in bulk.
new.folderHint: Choose one folder and discover its memory sources.
new.emptyHint: Create a project with no sources and configure it later.

scan.title: Scan Claude projects
scan.activateHint: Checking these creates a memory/ folder and starts syncing it.
scan.dupeName: Two selected projects share the name "{{name}}".

notebook.title: Notebook
notebook.sub: Notes and prompts stored in your memory repo, organized by project and a shared General space. Saved locally; synced with the rest.
notebook.emptyFolder: Empty — add a note or folder.
notebook.pickHint: Pick a note from the tree to view or edit it.
notebook.notConnectedHint: The Notebook lives in your synced repo. Set it up in Settings to start writing.
notebook.deleteBody: Delete "{{name}}"? This removes it from the Notebook on every machine after the next sync.

conflicts.explainer: For each file, choose which version to keep. Local = what's on this machine; Remote = what's in the repo.
conflicts.chooseEach: Choose a version for each file.

preflight.title: System requirements
preflight.sub: Claude Total Recall syncs your memory through a private GitHub repo, using git and the GitHub CLI (gh). Both need to be installed, and gh signed in to your GitHub account.

connect.title: Connect your memory repo
connect.sub: The private GitHub repo where your synced memory lives. Create an empty private repo — we suggest naming it claude-memories — then paste its URL below. Needs git and gh ready (previous step).
connect.connectedCreated: Repo connected and initial structure created.

register.title: Register this machine
register.sub: A logical id for this computer. If you leave it empty, we use the hostname.

firstProject.title: Add a project?
firstProject.sub: Optional. A project syncs specific memory folders, on top of your user config.

done.title: All set
done.sub: Your memory is ready to sync across machines.

pinned.title: Pinned files
pinned.sub: Single files synced globally, outside any project (for example a specific CLAUDE.md).
pinned.registerFirst: Register this machine first to pin files.
pinned.nameRequired: Give the pinned file a name.

deleteFile.title: Remove pinned file
deleteFile.body: Remove "{{pin}}" for all machines? It doesn't delete the file, it just stops syncing it.

preview.removed: This file was removed in this change — no preview.
preview.truncated: Large file — showing the first part only.
preview.parseError: Not valid JSON — showing the raw text.

memoryHelp.title: Reconcile your MEMORY.md index
memoryHelp.intro: MEMORY.md is the index of a memory store. When two machines held different memories and then synced, the index can end up reflecting only the machine that synced last — listing memories that aren't on disk, or missing ones that are.
memoryHelp.whenRun: If your machines had genuinely different memory content that just merged, run the maintenance pass below in Claude Code, in the affected project, to reconcile the index with the files on disk.
memoryHelp.whenIgnore: If this update is just a reconciliation you already ran on another machine propagating here, it's already consistent — you don't need to run anything. This notice appears whenever a MEMORY.md arrives from another machine, not only when something's wrong.
memoryHelp.paste: Paste this prompt into Claude Code, in the affected project:
